result.summary: 1Password exposes several developer integration surfaces: REST APIs for Events, Users for Partners, Partnership, and self-hosted Connect; a public documentation MCP server plus a local Environments MCP server; and the `op` CLI.
result.description: 1Password is a password manager and secrets-management platform for individuals, teams, and businesses. It provides tools for storing credentials, managing vaults and developer secrets, automating administrative tasks, and integrating secrets into applications and workflows.

events_bearer.label: 1Password Events API bearer token
events_bearer.setup: Create an Events API integration token either in the 1Password web app or with the CLI. Easiest path: sign in to [1Password](https://start.1password.com/signin), go to [Integrations Directory](https://start.1password.com/integrations/directory) / Events API integration setup as described in the [Events API get started docs](https://www.1password.dev/events-api/get-started), or run `op events-api create <name>` as documented in the [CLI events-api command reference](https://www.1password.dev/cli/reference/management-commands/events-api). Choose the needed features: `auditevents`, `itemusages`, and/or `signinattempts`. The token is shown once when created; store it securely.

users_oauth_app.label: 1Password Users API OAuth application client credentials
users_oauth_app.setup: Sign in to [1Password](https://start.1password.com/signin), open the [Integrations Directory](https://start.1password.com/integrations/directory), choose **OAuth Application**, configure the app, select the needed Users API scopes, then select **Generate credentials**. Save the resulting client ID and client secret as described in the [Users API authorization guide](https://www.1password.dev/users-api/authorization). Use those credentials to request short-lived access tokens from the Users API token endpoint via the OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow.

connect_token.label: 1Password Connect server access token
connect_token.setup: Create or open a Connect server Secrets Automation environment in [1Password Developer tools](https://start.1password.com/developer-tools/active), then issue a **New Token** from that Connect server as described in [Manage Connect servers](https://www.1password.dev/connect/manage-connect). You can also create a Connect server and first token with the CLI using `op connect server create <serverName> --vaults <vaultName>` per the [Connect get started guide](https://www.1password.dev/connect/get-started). Use the resulting token in applications that call your Connect server REST API.

partnership_bearer.label: 1Password Partnership API bearer token
partnership_bearer.setup: Register as a 1Password partner through the [1Password Partner Program](https://1password.com/partnerships) or contact the [1Password Partnerships team](mailto:partners@1password.com). Registered partners are granted bearer tokens for the Partnership API billing servers, as described in the [Partnership API reference](https://www.1password.dev/partnership-api/reference/).

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: Choose the regional API host documented for the account: `https://api.1password.com`, `https://api.1password.ca`, or `https://api.1password.eu`.

variables.name: account_id
variables.resolveFrom: Your 1Password account identifier used in resource paths like `/v1beta1/accounts/<account_id>/users`.
variables.description: Account ID path parameter for resource endpoints.

variables.name: your-connect-host
variables.resolveFrom: The hostname where you deployed your self-hosted Connect API container.

mechanics.command: op is not authenticated with Users API OAuth app credentials; this credential is not used by the CLI directly

variables.name: region
variables.resolveFrom: Choose the regional Events API host shown in the OpenAPI servers list: `events.1password.com`, `events.1password.ca`, `events.1password.eu`, or `events.ent.1password.com`.
